claim: Independent verification confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate target project files or lack required evidence scope declarations. The system uses provider-adapter abstraction to spawn Claude, Codex, or custom agents with configurable tool profiles while enforcing project-mutation boundaries through file digest snapshots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA256 digests for all project files excluding allowed write roots; diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after digest maps to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: Confirmed boundary detection logic matches explorer claim exactly via independent source read.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes boundary snapshot before agent execution (line 516), compares after execution (line 566-567), generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for changes outside allowed roots (lines 573-597), and prevents PRD readiness when violations occur.
evidence.why: Verified runtime enforcement flow matches claimed implementation via independent code inspection.

evidence.summary: execution_policy declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots: [artifact directory], documenting the boundary contract enforced at runtime.
evidence.why: Confirmed policy documentation of boundary contract matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes provider settings, handles agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full), applies claude-specific settings (no_session_persistence, permission mode), and clears conflicting tool restriction flags to avoid provider-specific conflicts.
evidence.why: Verified provider configuration logic matches claimed implementation.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes hard rules forbidding target project modification, requiring evidence scope declarations, and instructing providers to return JSON without writing artifacts themselves.
evidence.why: Confirmed agent prompt contract matches claimed restrictions.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence scope values to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Verified scope validation set matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type, with project scope requiring repo-relative paths or file locators.
evidence.why: Confirmed classification enforces evidence requirements for existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators or have only external/user/unknown evidence.
evidence.why: Verified enforcement prevents unverified assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers when evidence records omit the required scope field.
evidence.why: Confirmed validation prevents agents from submitting evidence without scope declaration.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check policy to force independent verification of high-risk claims.
evidence.why: Verified cross-check protocol prevents single-agent errors on risky factual claims.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific commands: Claude (claude -p with model, permission mode, settings), Codex (codex exec with sandbox, approval policy, output file), or custom (sh -c with command).
evidence.why: Confirmed provider abstraction enables spawning different AI providers with appropriate configurations.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes provider commands with timeout, captures stdout/stderr, reads Codex output files, and detects budget-exceeded failures.
evidence.why: Verified provider runtime handles evidence agent execution and failure classification.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker preventing PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Verified test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement works in practice.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary detects untracked and gitignored project mutations outside excluded directories like node_modules and dist.
evidence.why: Confirmed test evidence proves git-aware boundary detection handles real-world repo state.

evidence.summary: Codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox, and claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags, relying on boundary enforcement instead.
evidence.why: Verified test evidence proves provider-specific handling and boundary-based security model.

evidence.summary: Recent execution artifact shows boundary: clean with zero changes, all three agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) completed successfully with status: completed, and final readiness.prd_ready: true.
evidence.why: Verified execution evidence proves the system works end-to-end with real Claude agents.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect mutations in non-git repos (no git ls-files fallback), agents could corrupt projects without generating blockers.
If claude provider ignores agent_tool_profile restrictions at runtime and spawns full-tool agents despite boundary profile, the boundary audit becomes the only safety layer.
If evidence scope classification heuristics misclassify project paths as external due to ambiguous sources, valid evidence could be incorrectly rejected.

result.notes: All explorer claims independently verified via direct source reads of implementation files and execution artifact. Line numbers, function names, logic flows, test assertions, and recent dispatch results match exactly. No conflicts found. Boundary audit implementation is confirmed to exist, be tested, and successfully execute with clean boundary and all agents passing. Provider adapter correctly handles Claude/Codex/custom spawning with configurable tool profiles while relying on boundary enforcement rather than tool restriction flags for safety.
